posts.text:
The Islamic Republic has begun executing detainees from the January protests, despite previously assuring President Trump that executions of more than 800 protesters would be halted.
Today, three young  protesters were executed by hand in Iran.

This isn’t just a broken promise.
It’s a deliberate act of deception followed by deadly repression.

But no regime can build its future on the blood of its people without facing justice.

Mehdi Ghasemi,l
Saleh Mohammadi
M Saeed Davoudi

His name is Saleh Mohammadi, 19 years old wrestler. He was hanged today in Iran for the crime of protesting in last January and demanding💔

Video

Today, in Iran, in the middle of a war, the regime executed a 19-year-old national wrestling champion for the crime of joining January protests. 💔

After signaling to the world, including President @realDonaldTrump, that they would halt executions of protesters, the regime has done the exact opposite.

Three young protesters, Saleh Mohammadi, Mehdi Ghasemi, and Saeed Davoudi, were hanged in Qom after a sham trial.

Reports indicate torture. Forced confessions. No access to chosen lawyers. Closed

در حالی که به زودی وارد سال جدید خواهیم شد، جمهوری اسلامی سفره‌ی هفت‌سین خانواده‌های ایرانی را دوباره با خون رنگین کرد.

 سحرگاه ۲۸ اسفند ۱۴۰۴، ماشین کشتار حکومت سه تن از بازداشت‌شدگان اعتراضات ۱۸ و ۱۹ دی‌ماه، به نام‌های صالح محمدی، مهدی قاسمی و سعید داودی را در زندان قم به دار آویخت.

این اعدام‌های شتاب‌زده که تنها دو ماه پس از بازداشت صورت گرفت، نه اجرای عدالت، بلکه یک «انتقام حکومتی» عریان برای ایجاد رعب و وحشت در جامعه است.

​روند دادرسی این پرونده، نمایش تمام‌عیاری از نقض سیستماتیک حقوق بشر

Meet Yazdan Afrough.

He was 18. A wrestling champion from Tehran. A young athlete known for his strength, discipline, and the kind of determination that wins medals.

On January 19, in the Shadabad neighborhood of Tehran, security forces shot him.

He fell to the ground wounded.

And then they finished the job, two more bullets, to the heart and the stomach.

Afterward, his family was pressured to lie. They were told to say he was a member of the Basij. Human rights reports say otherwise: he wa

Meet Sahar Fard.

A respected taekwondo coach and referee. A woman who dedicated her life to sport, discipline, and training the next generation of athletes.

On January 18, in Eslamshahr, her life was taken.

Another athlete. Another coach. Another life devoted to sport, cut short in a country where even those who build strength and hope are treated as threats.

Say her name: Sahar Fard.

#IranMassacre‌

Meet Sahba Rashtian.

She was 23. A women’s football player in Iran’s premier league and a referee’s assistant on the field.

She was also an artist, a nature lover, and someone who lived life with the energy that athletes bring to everything they do.

On January 19, in the city of Isfahan, she was shot by the Islamic Republic’s security forces.

A direct bullet to her side ended the life of a young woman who had spent 23 years growing, dreaming, creating.

At her funeral, her mother held her ph

Meet Arnika Dabbagh.

A teenage swimmer from Gorgan. A national champion who dreamed of one day competing in the Olympics.

Her life was simple in the way great athletes’ lives are: early mornings, hard training, chasing a dream that required discipline and courage.

On January 19, she was shot during the protests.

And here’s the part that tells you everything about the system ruling Iran: when people tried to rush her to a hospital, they couldn’t even reach help because the regime had cut the

🚨 IMPORTANT – Flashing SOS

To the athletes of the world:  Iranian athletes were killed for demanding freedom. You have global platforms. Millions listen to you. Use that voice now to help Iranian to get rid of tyranny. Let’s be united against Sport washing. Let’s be united support Freedom.

Let me introduce you to heroes brave Iranian athletes who refused to stay silent. They went to the streets with empty hands demanding freedom, and many were shot in the heart or the head. Please join us and

"They will torture them. They will imprison them. They will rape them."

The Iranian regime threatened the families of the women's national team players who were in Australia.

That's why many rejected the political asylum offered to them and returned terrified to Iran.

They played a few days without a hijab. They breathed freedom. They smiled genuinely for the first time.

The soccer players before leaving Australia told me:

"We felt freedom for a couple of days… and now we have to go back."

“In a normal country, athletes get medals. In my country, they get bullets.”
Here’s a female soccer player who had to flee Iran, not for doping, not for cheating, but for playing without a piece of cloth on her head.

 cnn.com/2026/03/17/sport/shi…

Before some analysts in the West rush to rebrand him as a “moderate” or a “pragmatist,” it is important to establish who Esmail Khatib actually was.

I speak not as an observer, but as someone who has been arrested and interrogated by Iran’s Ministry of Intelligence and who has spent years documenting the testimonies of families whose loved ones were detained, tortured, or killed by this same institution.

Khatib served as Islamic Republic of Iran’s Minister of Intelligence, an agency that has l

Two photos;
One shows @BarbaraSlavin1’s smiling interview.
Two: thousands of victims and the man in the first photo ordered the massacre. 

That’s the reality behind the smiles.

 And now analysts, like Barbara, talk to media about the same killer Ali Larijani as if they are describing the leader of a democratic country.

She proudly says she interviewed him four times and even praises him as someone who supposedly wanted to resolve tensions with the United States.

That is simply not true.

Ali

در گفتگو با تلویزیون اسکای نیوز از انگلستان، از عملکرد سردبیر این رسانه انتقاد کردم و‌ گفتم درست در زمانی که خیابان‌های ایران هنوز بوی خون می‌داد و‌ در بیمارستان‌ها زخمی‌ها را کشتند و به پرستارها تجاوز کردند، سردبیر این رسانه رفت به سفارتخانه رژیم تا در جشن سالگرد پیروزی انقلاب اسلامی  شرکت کند به همراه نمایندگانی از نخست وزیر و دولت انگلیس.
خودتان بشنوید و قضاوت کنید. مهم نیست دیگر به این رسانه دعوت نشوم، بیان حقیقت مهمتر از راضی نگه داشتن این رسانه‌ها یا سیاستمداران است…


Masih Alinejad 🏳️ (@

Ayatollah Khamenei is hiding alone like a rat: Masih Alinejad

The Pain of 57 Mothers

Women Who Turned Pain into a Lifeline for Political Prisoners
Supporting freedom fighters is not an act of kindness.
